flagship_directive: Prove a matching feasible-minimax lower bound showing that, up to the same graph-only envelope scale or a sharp constant, no heterogeneous Bernoulli design and Hajek-type graph-only conservative procedure can uniformly improve on the optimized envelope over bounded all-treated/all-control potential outcomes.
critique: The note proves a genuine heterogeneous-Bernoulli bipartite design result: an exact covariance identity for the Hajek linearization, a graph-only conservative variance envelope, convex envelope optimization under budget and positivity constraints, and pointwise CLT/Wald validity for the optimized design under bounded-degree dependency conditions. The delivered design criterion is conservative rather than minimax-sharp: there is no converse showing that the envelope, the optimized envelope value, or the resulting interval length is unavoidable over bounded potential outcomes. The surrogate certificate is proved only as a constant-factor sandwich controlled by the bounded outcome degree and positivity floor, so it is useful but not a sharp or broadly adaptive design theory. This clears a field-level contribution, but the headline should not be read as a solved minimax theory beyond the proved conservative envelope-optimal program and pointwise inference theorem.
